steps.title: Pin down the key evidence
steps.content: The passage says early quasars formed “without the need for ultraviolet backgrounds or other extreme and implausible environmental conditions that models of the early universe have included.” The new research shows that conditions previous models treated as necessary were not actually needed — those assumptions were unfounded.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — it may be possible to develop models of the early universe that rest on more credible assumptions than previous models have.: Directly follows from the evidence: if the old assumptions were implausible and unnecessary, new models can be built on better-supported assumptions. ✓
  • Choice B — future models of the early universe may not need to include conditions that explain early quasars since those quasars may not have actually existed at the time.: The passage never casts doubt on whether early quasars existed; it explains how they formed. ✗
  • Choice C — previous models of the early universe may have underemphasized the importance of ultraviolet backgrounds to quasar formation.: Reverses the logic — the passage says ultraviolet backgrounds were overemphasized (treated as necessary when they were not). ✗
  • Choice D — factors that previous models of the early universe assumed were necessary for the formation of quasars may have actually been consequences of the formation of quasars.: Invents a cause-consequence reversal not stated or implied by the passage. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
